untranslatable_reason: Signal requires backtesting win rate analysis across multiple timeframes (52% threshold validation) and historical pattern matching (trend/volatility/price structure characteristics). These are meta-level system validation requirements that cannot be expressed as point-in-time indicator conditions. Win rate calculation requires aggregate statistical analysis across a dataset, not a single-bar rule. Additionally, 'common characteristic across great historical moves' is qualitative and not quantifiable with available indicators.
translation_notes: This signal describes a system development methodology (testing reliability across timeframes) rather than entry/exit rules. It requires: (1) Multi-timeframe win rate backtesting, (2) Statistical reliability threshold (52%), and (3) Qualitative pattern matching. These cannot be translated into real-time indicator conditions for a backtest engine. The rule is a meta-strategy filter, not a tradeable signal. To make this actionable, one would need to first run a full backtest to calculate win rates, then apply those results as a pre-filter before deploying any specific entry/exit rules.
